steps.title: Identify the decisive contrast
steps.content: The researchers found bulkheads are more strongly negatively correlated with waterbird community integrity than riprap is—meaning a site with high bulkheads should have lower IWCI scores than a site with high riprap.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content:
Waterbird communities at Old Road, a site with a relatively high percentage of shoreline consisting of bulkheads, had lower average IWCI scores than did waterbird communities at Miles, a site with a relatively high percentage of shoreline consisting of riprap. directly demonstrates this: Old Road (high bulkheads) has lower IWCI scores than Miles (high riprap).

The other three options either compare sites with identical or mixed hardening types that don't clearly isolate the bulkhead-riprap difference, or measure overall hardening without distinguishing between types.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
